liverefresh.description: Indicates whether live refresh of cache is supported for this property.
liverefresh.title: Live Refresh

maxtype.description: Maximo data type for values of this property (ALN, INTEGER, YORN)
maxtype.title: Data Type

masked.description: Identified whether this value is masked in the System Properties application
masked.title: Masked

encryptedvalue.description: Encrypted property value.  Will be populated on the database when MaxProp.Encrypted is True.
encryptedvalue.title: Encrypted Value

changeby.description: The User ID who last changed this row.
changeby.title: Changed By

changedate.description: The timestamp of the last change applied to this row.
changedate.title: Change Date

serverhost.description: The IP address of this server instance.  This will usually be null.  When server host is null, any server with the specified server name will use this property.  When server host is not null, the property will be used only by an instance having the same server name and IP address.
serverhost.title: Server Host

servername.description: Server name.  For global property values, this will equal COMMON.
servername.title: Server

propvalue.description: Property value (unencrypted).  Will be populated on the database when MaxProp.Encrypted is False.
propvalue.title: Value

domainid.description: The validation domain for values of this property.
domainid.title: Domain

globalonly.description: Indicates whether this property must exist only at a system-wide level.  If true, this property cannot have an instance-specific value.
globalonly.title: Global Only

instanceonly.description: Indicates whether this property must be defined only at the instance level.  If true, an instance-specific value must be defined for each servername and there will be no global value.
instanceonly.title: Instance Only

nullsallowed.description: Indicates whether a null value is permitted on MaxPropValue for this property.
nullsallowed.title: Nulls Allowed

onlinechanges.description: Indicates whether the user is allowed to override the value via the Property application.
onlinechanges.title: Online Changes Allowed

changeby.description: The User ID who last changed this row.

changedate.description: The timestamp of the last changed applied to this row.

encrypted.description: Indicates whether the value of the property should be encrypted when stored on the MaxPropValue table.

securelevel.description: Indicates the level of access permitted for this property

maximodefault.description: The default value used in a new Maximo installation.  This supports refreshing all properties with the system defaults.

userdefined.description: Indicates whether this property was delivered with Maximo or was defined by a user.

RESOURCE_MXAPIAPPPROPCFG.description: Maximo API for Configuring Application Properties
RESOURCE_MXAPIAPPPROPCFG.title: APPPROPCFG

RESOURCE_MXAPILAUNCHPOINT.description: Maximo API for Script Launch Point
RESOURCE_MXAPILAUNCHPOINT.title: SCRIPTLAUNCHPOINT

add.description: If selected, the script is activated by the add event. The launch point must have at least one activation event, and you can select multiple events.
add.title: Add

overridden.description: Indicates if the value for the variable has been overridden
overridden.title: Overridden

varname.description: Name of the variable used within the launch point
varname.title: Variable

objectname.description: The object that launches the script.
objectname.title: Object

update.description: If selected, the script is activated by the update event. The launch point must have at least one activation event, and you can select multiple events.
update.title: Update

attributename.description: Specify the attribute associated with the launch point.
attributename.title: Attribute

actionname.description: The action takes its name from the launch point, or you can modify the action name with the name of an existing action. When you create the script, a new action is saved in the database, and you can configure the action in the Actions application.
actionname.title: Action

delete.description: If selected, the script is activated by the delete event. The launch point must have at least one activation event, and you can select multiple events.
delete.title: Delete

objectevent.description: Specify the event associated with the launch point
objectevent.title: Event

launchpointtype.description: Specifies the type of launch point
launchpointtype.title: Launch Point Type

condition.description: A filter that identifies the set of records for the business object that is targeted by this script. The filter is expressed in the form of an SQL WHERE clause. You can enter this condition manually, or click the Detail Menu button to use the SQL Expression Builder.
condition.title: Object Event Condition

dispvalue.description: Specify a display value for the action based on the action type.
dispvalue.title: Value

description_longdescription.description: Describes the action.The description can be up to 255 characters in length. To enter or view additional information, click the Long Description button.
description_longdescription.title: Details

value2.description: Field to store value for type APPACTION only.  This field is translatable.
value2.title: Value

objectname.description: Type of business objects on which an action can be applied.
objectname.title: Object

description.description: Describes the action. Actions are associated to escalation points by action groups.
description.title: Description

memo.description: A memo briefly describing why the status was changed, which is placed into the status table entry that records the status change for the business object.
memo.title: Memo

type.description: Displays categories of actions. MAXIMO ships with a number of predefined action types, such as Set Owner, Status Change, or Create Ticket.
type.title: Type

usewith.description: Indicate from where action is accessible.
usewith.title: Accessible From

value.description: An action string of an action, describes the outcome of an action.
value.title: Value

ACTION_SAVETHISQUERY.description: Request Schema for save this query

clausename.description: The unique name of the Where clause that generates the query.
clausename.title: Query Name

userid.description: User for which quesry is shown.
userid.title: User

RESOURCE_MXAPIQUERYRECSTATE.description: Maximo API for Queries Record State
RESOURCE_MXAPIQUERYRECSTATE.title: USERQUERY

clausename.description: The unique name of the Where clause that generates the query.
clausename.title: Query Name

userid.description: User for which quesry is shown.
userid.title: User

apptrequired.description: Indicates if an appointment is required for the work order. Tasks that are created for this work order inherit this value, but can be modified.
apptrequired.title: Appointment Required

estservcost.description: Total estimated service cost against this work order.
estservcost.title: Estimated Service Cost

parentchgsstatus.description: Flags whether this work order should change its status when the parents status changes.
parentchgsstatus.title: Inherit Status Changes

ownergroup.description: The group currently responsible for this record.
ownergroup.title: Owner Group

startdate.description: The datetime someone was assigned to start this requirement
startdate.title: Start Date

assetlocpriority.description: Asset priority copied from ASSET table.
assetlocpriority.title: Asset Location Priority

genforpolineid.description: POLINEID number that required the work
genforpolineid.title: PO Line ID

estatapprmatcost.description: Estimate at time of Approval of all materials cost for the WO
estatapprmatcost.title: Estimate Material Cost at Approval

taskid.description: ID number of the task work order. Maximo sometimes appends the Task ID to the parent work order number. For example, tasks for work order 100 might be numbered 100-10, 100-20, 100-30, etc.
taskid.title: Task

pmduedate.description: From the original Next Due Date of the PM
pmduedate.title: PM Due Date

workdate.description: The day that labor availability was calculated for. This date can be different from the assignment dates if the shift crosses midnight.
workdate.title: Work Date

pluscloop.description: Select this check box to identify the work order as a loop calibration. May be selected when a value is entered in the Location field.
pluscloop.title: Loop Calibration

fnlconstraint.description: The date that work should be completed by.  Scheduled dates should be before this date.
fnlconstraint.title: Finish No Later Than

laborcode.description: Code for the labor or craft that performs the operation.
laborcode.title: Labor

estatapprtoolcost.description: Estimate at time of Approval of all tools cost for the WO
estatapprtoolcost.title: Estimate Tool Cost at Approval

hasfollowupwork.description: Indicates if there is follow up work for this work order
hasfollowupwork.title: Follow-up Work

intshift.description: Specifies the shift on which the work will be interrupted in the Graphical Scheduling application.
intshift.title: Interruptible shift

phone.description: The phone number of the person reporting the work order
phone.title: Phone

actservcost.description: Total actual service cost against this work order.
actservcost.title: Actual Service Cost

amcrew.description: Identifies the crew that performed the work.
amcrew.title: Crew

pointnum.description: The point number for which this measurement is being taken.
pointnum.title: Measurement Point

commodity.description: Defines the service commodity for this Work Order.
commodity.title: Service

commoditygroup.description: Defines the Commodity Service Group for the Work Order.
commoditygroup.title: Service Group

reportedby.description: The person reporting this work order
reportedby.title: Reported By

pluscoverduedate.description: Displays the over due date for the calibration, if the work order is not completed.
pluscoverduedate.title: Calibration Overdue Date

pmextdate.description: From the Extended Date of the PM
pmextdate.title: PM Extension Date

reasonforchange.description: Defines the reason for the Change
reasonforchange.title: Reason for Change

skilllevel.description: Skill level assigned to the craft.
skilllevel.title: Skill Level

pluscjprevnum.description: Displays the revision number of the Job Plan the work order was generated with.
pluscjprevnum.title: Job Plan Revision Number

assetnum.description: Asset on which work is done. If Maximo generates a work order from a PM, Maximo copies the asset from the PM record.
assetnum.title: Asset

crewid.description: ID for a given crew within an craft
crewid.title: Crew

contractnum.description: Contract number for an outside rate.
contractnum.title: Contract

calendar.description: Calendar to determine shift work is to be done on
calendar.title: Calendar

onbehalfof.description: This work order has been created on behalf of this person.
onbehalfof.title: Requested on Behalf Of

observation.description: Describes an inspection observation of asset or location by an inspector
observation.title: Observation

finishdate.description: The datetime someone was assigned to finish this requirement
finishdate.title: Finish Date

interruptible.description: Specifies whether the work order is allowed to be stopped once the work has begun and then restarted. If the check box is selected, the work can be stopped. If the check box is cleared, the work cannot be stopped and restarted.
interruptible.title: Interruptible

remdur.description: Estimated time to complete work order
remdur.title: Time Remaining

downtime.description: Does the Asset have to be down for this work order?
downtime.title: Downtime

whomischangefor.description: Identifies whom a change is for.
whomischangefor.title: Whom is this change for

worklog.description: Indicates if work log exists for the assignment.
worklog.title: WORKLOG

parentassignid.description: The AssignmentID of the assignment this assignment was split off from.
parentassignid.title: Parent Assignment ID

repairfacility.description: Specifies the repair facility location. A repair facility can take ownership of work orders from multiple sites in the same organization. User security can be configured to give permission to view work orders in multiple sites if the work orders are owned by a single repair facility.
repairfacility.title: Repair Facility

assnapptreq.description: Indicates if an appointment is required for the assignment.
assnapptreq.title: Appointment Required

estatapprlabhrs.description: Estimate at time of Approval of all the labor hours for the WO
estatapprlabhrs.title: Estimate Labor Hours at Approval

estatapprservcost.description: Estimate at time of Approval of service cost for the WO
estatapprservcost.title: Estimate Service Cost at Approval

respondby.description: A Calculated field specifying when by a breakdown should be responded to.
respondby.title: Respond By

craft.description: Code for the craft listed in the Labor List.
craft.title: Craft

scheduledate.description: Date and time the work is scheduled to begin.
scheduledate.title: Scheduled Date

appointment.description: Indicates if an appointment has been scheduled.
appointment.title: Appointment Made

estatapprlabcost.description: Estimate at time of Approval of the labor costs for the WO
estatapprlabcost.title: Estimate Labor Cost at Approval

origrecordid.description: Defines the source record used to create this record.
origrecordid.title: Originating Record

pmnextduedate.description: From the next Next Due Date of the PM
pmnextduedate.title: PM Next Due Date

worklocation.description: Identifier of the Work Location that is to be responsible for the work.
worklocation.title: Work Location

verification.description: Used to verify that the Change was successful or describe how to determine if the Change was successful
verification.title: Verification

woacceptscharges.description: Does this work order accept charges?
woacceptscharges.title: Accepts Charges

calcpriority.description: Priority level used for scheduling the work order. Values from 0-999 are valid. The default value is either NULL or the priority established in the job plan, PM, or route assigned to the work order.
calcpriority.title: Calculated Priority

chargestore.description: Indicates whether charges on the WO should be added to the asset.
chargestore.title: Charge to Store

woclass.description: The class of the work order record.
woclass.title: Work Order Class

backoutplan.description: Describes the back out plan in the event the change needs to be reversed.
backoutplan.title: Back Out Plan

justifypriority.description: Describes the justification for the priority given to this work order, change or release.
justifypriority.title: Priority Justification

laborhrs.description: Number of hours required to complete an assignment. When an edit to the start date/time, regular hours, or overtime hours results in a total number of hours longer than the time span between the start date/time and the finish date/time, Maximo recalculates the finish date/time.
laborhrs.title: Labor Hours

wovendor.description: Defines the Vendor responsible for the work order.
wovendor.title: Vendor

inctasksinsched.description: Indicates that this work order's tasks will be included in the schedule view.
inctasksinsched.title: Include Tasks in Schedule

wplaborid.description: ID to identify the EXACT WPLABOR record used to generate this row.
wplaborid.title: WP Labor ID

actstart.description: Actual Start date of this work order
actstart.title: Actual Start

reportdate.description: The date work order was reported
reportdate.title: Reported Date

assignstatus.description: Status of the assignment. When the status changes, Maximo sets the status date to the current date/time.
assignstatus.title: Assignment Status

sneconstraint.description: The earliest date at which work should start.  Scheduled dates should be after this date.
sneconstraint.title: Start No Earlier Than

wosequence.description: Indicates sequence to execute Work Orders of a given WO hierarchy
wosequence.title: WO Sequence

owner.description: The person currently responsible for this record.
owner.title: Owner

measuredate.description: Date/Time when measurement was taken or when observation was taken
measuredate.title: Measurement Date

environment.description: This field defines the environment in which the change is taking place.
environment.title: Environment

failurecode.description: Indicates Top level of a Failure Hierarchy
failurecode.title: Failure Class

pluscphyloc.description: Enter a detailed description of the location of the asset or specify the location information using a hierarchy that you created. Using a hierarchy provides consistent identification and groups assets efficiently.
pluscphyloc.title: Physical Location

location.description: Location of the asset or the location where work was done. If Maximo generates a work order from a PM, maximo copies the location from the PM record.
location.title: Location

risk.description: Defines the risk level of the work order, change or release.
risk.title: Risk Assessment

actfinish.description: Actual Finish date of this work order
actfinish.title: Actual Finish

haschildren.description: Y if WO has children N if it has no children
haschildren.title: Has Children

repfacsiteid.description: The site for the repair facility.
repfacsiteid.title: Repair Facility Site

description.description: Describes the Where clause that is used to generate the query.
description.title: Description

clausename.description: The unique name of the Where clause that generates the query.
clausename.title: Query Name

ispublic.description: Indicates whether the query is available to be used by all users or only by the creator.
ispublic.title: Public

401.description:
BMXAA0033I Your session timed out due to inactivity.
BMXAA0026E The action could not be performed because the application server security is enabled. Proper authorization is required to perform this action. Report this error to your system administrator.
BMXAA7901E You cannot log in at this time. Contact the system administrator.
BMXAA0025E The method cannot be called because application server security is not enabled. Enable Application Server Security. For details, see information on defining server security in the information center.
BMXAA4122E You cannot be connected to an application server on the specified host. Ensure that an application server is running.
BMXAA0020E The session that is used to access business objects is either not valid or has timed out. Verify that the remote system is avaialble, then log in and authenticate again.
BMXAA0021E User name and password combination are not valid. Try again.
BMXAA0011E User {0} is inactive. Specify a different user.

412.description: BMXAA8229W Record {0} has been updated by another user. Your changes have not been saved. Refresh the record and try again.

413.description: BMXAA2249E The file being attached exceeds the size limit of {0}MB. See your system administrator to have the limit adjusted.

403.description:
BMXAA1671I Skip transaction.
BMXAA2283E Your password has expired.

404.description:
BMXAA4216E Unknown Object named {0} has been encountered. Gather the logs from the directory and determine the cause and source of the error.
BMXAA9510E The stable page resource was not found because the reload of a stable page was attempted or the idle expiry time has expired.
BMXAA4187E The relationship {0} does not exist for business object {1}. Verify that the relationship specified in parameter {0} exists in the Maxrelationship table.
BMXAA1283E The {0} value in the Consumed By field cannot be used. Define an object structure with a Consumed By value that is valid for the processing action.
BMXAA8727E The OSLC resource {0} with the ID {1} was not found as it does not exist in the system. In the database, verify whether the resource for the ID exists.

415.description: BMXAA8823E The format {0} is not supported. You must use a supported format such as application/json.

405.description: BMXAA8815E The HTTP method DELETE is not permitted on a collection resource. Only HTTP GET and POST methods are supported on collection URIs.

409.description: BMXAA9524E The transaction ID {1} already exists in the OSLC transaction table with resource ID {2} for object structure {3}.

opmodeor.name: opmodeor
opmodeor.description: if set to 1, this will cause the oslc.where clause to set the query mode to OR clause instead of AND.

oslc.properties.name: oslc.properties
oslc.properties.description: Select clause for selecting a partial view of the member resource (like an individual workorder).

transactionid.name: transactionid
transactionid.description: This is used to prevent de-duplication for create/update/delete. If this header is present then the value is compared to a list of committed transaction ids.If it has a match, then it is considered as a duplicate transaction and an HTTP conflict error is generated.

oslc.where.name: oslc.where
oslc.where.description: Where clause to filter the set of resources

x-allow-events.name: x-allow-events
x-allow-events.description: For a create/update/delete, if the request wants to suppress the object events, this request header needs to be set to 0. By default all events are fired unless programmatically stopped.

x-method-override.name: x-method-override
x-method-override.description: Due to constraints from browser the HTTP POST method is re-used for creates and updates and deletes.This request header is used to convey the specifics of the POST request. PATCH is used for update. DELETE is used for deleting the resource. BULK is used for supporting bulk operations.If this header is not present, the POST request is treated as a create request.

gbrange.name: gbrange
gbrange.description: The range clause for the group by query.

lean.name: lean
lean.description: If set to 1, this will instruct the api to work with the "lean" json format as opposed to the namespaced json that it produces by default. This option is supported only starting 7601 release of the Maximo base. Its preferred to use this format as it reduces the size of the json payload.

oslc.select.name: oslc.select
oslc.select.description:
Filter clause following the OSLC query syntax
(see below for more details on this)

gbcols.name: gbcols
gbcols.description: The comma separated group by columns.

content-type.name: content-type
content-type.description: The mime-type of the request document.

gbsortby.name: gbsortby
gbsortby.description: The order by clause for the group by query. Follows the same syntax as oslc.orderBy.

id.name: id
id.description: Unique id for object structure resource

collectioncount.name: collectioncount
collectioncount.description: If set to 1, it will instruct the api to include the total count of records that matched the query ( responseInfo.totalCount). By default it will not return that unless the maximo property mxe.oslc.collectioncount is set to 1. This is a recommended way to control collection count at the request level.

oslc.searchTerms.name: oslc.searchTerms
oslc.searchTerms.description: Record wide text search for tokens specified as this query parameter value. Example oslc.searchTerms="pump","broken"

oslc.paging.name: oslc.paging
oslc.paging.description: Instruct the server to page the query response based on the default page size. The default page size needs to be configured on the server side for this to take effect.

apikey.name: apikey
apikey.description: Set to the users apikey. This is used when the 'api' context is used (instead of 'oslc'). Do make sure that the apikey is generated for the user. You can use the Administration app to generate an apikey

oslc.pageSize.name: oslc.pageSize
oslc.pageSize.description: Instruct the server to page the query result with the specified page size.

stablepaging.name: stablepaging
stablepaging.description: If set to true, will instruct the server to page the resource from a in session MboSet.

accept.name: accept
accept.description: The mime-type of the accept document.

patchtype.name: patchtype
patchtype.description: This request header with the value of MERGE will instruct the server to treat a PATCH request (POST with x-method-override as PATCH) processor to merge the child objects (array of child objects) in the request with the corresponding child elements in the server. If this header is not present, the server will delete the child objects that are missing from the request.

properties.name: properties
properties.description: Select clause for selecting a partial view of the member resource.
